The Numbers Don't Lie

This isn't a niche complaint.
It's an industry-wide blind spot.

3–8%

of total incentive payouts are lost to errors in manual, spreadsheet-driven compensation processes.

Gartner
83%

of organizations using spreadsheets for incentive comp experience recurring calculation errors.

Ventana Research
~28%

of a sales rep's week is actually spent selling — the rest lost to admin, including shadow commission tracking.

Salesforce, State of Sales
1.5×

a rep's annual salary — the conservative cost to replace them when pay disputes and mistrust drive top talent out.

SHRM / industry benchmark

Every figure points to the same gap: comp is one of the largest controllable line items in the enterprise — and one of the least governed.

The Gap
The Visibility Gap

Your biggest behavioral lever — the money you pay people to sell — runs with no feedback loop. You know what you paid. You don't know what it changed.

Gartner attributes 3–8% of incentive payouts to manual-process error alone — before a single behavioral question is even asked.

Unmeasured spend, this session
₹0
ticking up while you read — because nothing is watching it
One root cause The Visibility Gap
Leadership Precision & governance lost
Incentive Leakage Can't tell which payouts changed behavior — a strategic blind spot at budget scale.
Lagging Indicators By the time attainment data shows the problem, the quarter is already gone.
Performance Plateaus The gap between top reps and the rest never closes — the intelligence was never captured.
Sales Reps Trust & momentum lost
Distrust They don't trust the payout, so they rebuild it in shadow spreadsheets instead of selling.
Disengagement Not knowing where they stand, they've no reason to push for the next tier.
Disputes Every cycle becomes a fight with Finance — an internal tax on time and trust.
Two very different people · feeling the same broken system from opposite ends
~10%

The ones raising queries? They're your top performers — the people who track every rupee and escalate straight to the MD when it looks wrong.

So the real question isn't "how much did we spend?"
It's "what if everyone could finally see it?"

The Result

Growth

Leadership gets precision control and audit-proof governance. Sales reps get a real-time earnings GPS. One source of truth — adopted in three stages.
The 3C Maturity Model

Three stages. One cumulative climb
from governance to growth.

The 3Cs aren't separate products — each tier builds on the one before it. Start where you need to, expand as you mature. You can't drive effort you can't govern, and you can't coach a floor that doesn't trust its numbers.

C1
Stage 01 · Foundation

Compliance

Trust the Payout

Replace manual, error-prone spreadsheets with an auditable system of record. Incentives can only drive behavior once the foundation is accurate and governed.

  • No-Code Incentive Plan Designer
  • Automated Commission Computation
  • CRM / ERP Sync
  • Audit-Proof Governance
  • Dispute Management System
  • Payout Dashboards
3–8%of payouts recovered from manual errors

Why the sequence works
Two journeys, one outcome

Two arcs that become one.

Leadership travels from control to growth. The sales rep travels from trust to growth. Kennect runs both on one platform — and the moment they converge, the whole organisation compounds.

LeadershipControl
Alignment
Sales RepTrust

they sync
Growth one shared outcome
Control → Alignment+Trust → Clarity→Growth

"You cannot motivate someone who doesn't trust the system. You cannot coach someone who isn't connected to their goals." — when both arcs align, growth is the result.
